message:
perf(resilience,ratelimit,health,tracker,checkpoint,redaction): async persistence, ring buffers, and allocation reduction

Resilience monitor improvements:
- Health checks now copy session name under RLock and run the
  (potentially slow) health check function without holding the lock.
  Previously, checkHealth() held the lock during I/O which could
  block agent state reads from the API.
- Rate limit persistence (recordRateLimitHit/Success) now runs
  SaveToDir() asynchronously via goroutine+WaitGroup instead of
  blocking the health check loop. Disk I/O during rate limit events
  was adding 10-50ms latency to each health check cycle.
- restartAgent() now increments RestartCount only after a successful
  restart (previously incremented before the restart attempt, then
  decremented on failure -- a race condition if two health checks
  ran concurrently).
- Register Cursor/Windsurf/Aider/Ollama agent types in
  ScanAndRegisterAgents() command template lookup.

Rate limit tracker history merge:
- LoadFromDir() now merges history events from disk with in-memory
  events instead of overwriting. This prevents data loss when two
  processes (e.g., NTM server and resilience monitor) both persist
  to the same directory. Deduplication uses 1-second timestamp
  proximity + same action. Results re-sorted and capped at 100.
- Add Ollama to NormalizeProvider() mapping.
- DetectRateLimit() now calls DetectAllErrorsInOutput() once instead
  of calling both DetectErrorInOutput() and DetectAllErrorsInOutput()
  separately, eliminating redundant output scanning.

Health check simplification:
- checkAgent() now checks rate limit detection first (authoritative
  source) and only appends non-rate-limit issues from detectErrors().
  Previously, both code paths could add duplicate rate_limit issues.
- Use util.SafeSliceFromEnd() for the recent-output window in
  detectProgress() instead of manual bounds checking.

State tracker ring buffer:
- StateTracker now uses a true circular buffer with cursor+full fields
  instead of copy-shifting on every Record(). The old approach
  allocated a new slice every time the buffer was full, causing
  O(n) copy + GC pressure at ~100 entries per agent per minute.
- Since() and All() now iterate in insertion order using modular
  arithmetic from the cursor position.
- pruneOld() skips pruning when the buffer is full (relies on maxSize
  for memory bound, filters by age during query).

FileChangeStore lock optimization:
- Since() and All() now use RLock instead of Lock, allowing concurrent
  reads from the API server and dashboard.

Checkpoint git patch fix:
- generateGitPatch() now accepts a repoDir parameter and uses
  git -C to run in the correct directory. Previously it ran git diff
  in the current working directory, which could be wrong if NTM
  was launched from a different path.

Redaction performance:
- deduplicateMatches() replaces the byte-position bitmap (which
  allocated a []bool the size of the largest match end position) with
  direct overlap checking against already-kept matches. For typical
  inputs this is faster (small number of matches) and uses O(k) memory
  instead of O(n).
- applyRedactions() now uses strings.Builder with forward iteration
  instead of reverse-order string concatenation. The old approach
  created O(k) intermediate strings; the new approach makes one pass
  with a pre-grown builder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
